title: General Police Interaction & Arrest Rights
rights: Right to know the grounds of arrest (Section 50, CrPC)
Right to be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours (Section 57, CrPC)
Right to consult and be defended by a legal practitioner of your choice
Right to inform a relative or friend about the arrest
Right to free legal aid if you cannot afford a lawyer (NALSA guidelines)
avoid: Do not argue aggressively or use physical force against an officer
Do not run away if stopped for questioning
Do not sign any document or confession without reading it or consulting a lawyer

title: Women's Rights During Arrest
rights: Women can generally only be arrested by a female police officer
Women cannot be arrested after sunset and before sunrise (except in exceptional circumstances with a magistrate's written order)
Right to be searched only by another woman with strict regard to decency
avoid: Do not submit to an illegal nighttime arrest without asking for the magistrate's warrant
